org.name: Quill
org.description: A type-safe query builder and migration toolkit

selected.title: quill v6.0.0
selected.content: Major release. The connection pool is rewritten to release handles eagerly under load, which cuts steady-state memory on busy services. BREAKING: the implicit `any` row type is gone — queries now infer row shapes from the schema, and untyped raw queries must opt in with `raw<T>()`. The deprecated `Model.find()` helper is removed in favor of `query().where()`.

selected.title: quill-migrate v2.0.0
selected.content: Migrations can now run inside a single transaction with automatic rollback on failure, so a half-applied migration no longer leaves the schema wedged. Adds a `--dry-run` flag that prints the SQL without executing it. Generated migration files now include a checksum so drift between the file and the applied state is detected on the next run.

selected.title: quill v6.0.1
selected.content: Patch. Fixes a regression where `raw<T>()` lost the inferred type when chained after `.limit()`. Restores the prior behavior and adds a type-level test to guard it.

selected.title: Postgres 18 support
selected.content: Adds first-class support for Postgres 18, including the new `MERGE ... RETURNING` syntax and virtual generated columns. The driver negotiates protocol features at connect time, so older servers keep working unchanged.
